title: Chemistry
description: Steph’s battling chronic depression. Jamie’s crashed after pushing himself way too far, way too fast. They meet in a psychiatrist’s waiting room - hardly the stuff that rom-coms are made of - but the spark is instant and undeniable. What follows is messy, electric and unexpectedly beautiful. When your own brain feels like the enemy, is falling in love the bravest thing you can do - or the most reckless? Studies show that 1 in 4 adults in England will experience a mental health problem each year. Darkly funny and deeply moving, Jacob Marx Rice's Chemistry is a frank exploration of how we manage love - and life - when we rely on chemicals to keep us balanced.

reviews.excerpt: Genre: American Theater , Contemporary , Dark Comedy , Drama , Fringe Theatre , New Writing , Short Plays , Theatre , Tragedy Venue: Grania Dean Studio, The Lantern Theatre, Brighton

reviews.reviewer: Lela Tredwell FROM THE WRITER OF BEST NEW PLAY AT BRIGHTON FRINGE 2019 AND WINNE
reviews.excerpt: FROM THE WRITER OF BEST NEW PLAY AT BRIGHTON FRINGE 2019 AND WINNER OF BRIGHTON FRINGE’S OUSTANDING SHOW AWARD 2021 From the writer of theatrical hit Clean, comes this excellent short play, exploring the price of isolation and the importance of human touch.
